response.justification: The candidate is a highly experienced generalist with genuine developer-tooling interest — including hands-on IDE work on NI LabWindows/CVI and authoring test runners and libraries — but the core stack for this role is largely absent. His career centers on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang; Java appears only peripherally, and there is no evidence of Kotlin, Spring, JVM internals, or enterprise framework experience, which are hard requirements for building enterprise Java/Kotlin IDE tooling.
strengths: Direct IDE development experience (NI LabWindows/CVI components in C/C++)
Strong developer-tooling track record (test runners, assertion libraries, MCP code generators)
Deep systems experience with low-latency, high-throughput services and craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD)
concerns: No demonstrable Kotlin or Spring ecosystem experience — both required qualifications
Java exposure is minimal and dated; no evidence of deep JVM understanding
Overlapping employment dates across many roles make the timeline hard to verify
